summary: Reviewed implementation commit 2b151efc79ca730375978cd588a49972d8d6bdc3 on codex/m7-decision-inspector-rewrite. The rewrite mostly satisfies the decision-assistant structure: the right inspector now leads with current problem, recommendation, cost, benefit, risk, next action, and localized reason chips before grouped population/labor/grain/cash/route/governance/obligation/appointment/campaign data. Existing preview/confirmation paths are reused and no forbidden domain command path was introduced. Gameplay review still requests R2 before QA because the blocked-route recommendation can point the player to a disabled action, and the default player inspector contains newly added internal read-model copy.

tests_run.result: PASS; branch is codex/m7-decision-inspector-rewrite and was clean before writing this gameplay handoff.

PASS; reviewed implementation commit touching packages/ui/src/index.tsx, packages/ui/src/i18n.ts, apps/web/src/styles/shell.css, apps/web/src/shell/web-client-shell.test.tsx, and tests/e2e/web-shell.spec.ts.
PASS; subsequent branch changes are process/task/handoff routing files only, not implementation code.
PASS; no whitespace errors in process commits after reviewed implementation.
PASS; implementation commit has no whitespace errors.
PASS; format, lint, typecheck, and architecture boundary checks passed.

tests_run.command: manual gameplay/UX inspection of packages/ui/src/index.tsx and packages/ui/src/i18n.ts against the task acceptance criteria
tests_run.result: REQUEST_CHANGES; structure and command-boundary behavior are acceptable, but blocked-route next action and player-facing internal copy need R2.

decisions: Accepted as directionally correct that the right inspector now puts the decision assistant before raw metrics and groups existing data under resources, route/campaign, governance/appointment, effects, and actions.
Accepted that population, labor, grain, cash, route, governance, obligation, appointment, and campaign data remain reachable after the decision summary.
Accepted that reason chips in the inspected player path use i18n.formatReasonCode and the added stable reason keys hide the reviewed raw reason codes in default player mode.
Accepted that appointment and campaign actions still use existing preview/confirmation callbacks; no copied sim-core rule authority or new command path was found in the implementation diff.

Requested R2 because packages/ui/src/index.tsx:1809-1817 recommends Review obligations for an unreachable route, while packages/ui/src/index.tsx:1990-2007 disables the Review obligations button for the same unreachable-route state. A decision assistant cannot name a disabled control as the next action without giving the player an executable alternative.

Requested R2 because packages/ui/src/i18n.ts:112-113 and :568 add player-visible copy saying raw district read-model values. That is implementation language in the default right inspector and works against the product-owner complaint that the UI is not understandable or game-like.
risks: If the blocked-route mismatch ships to QA, QA can pass structural tests while players still see an instruction they cannot execute.

The i18n fallback still title-cases unknown reason codes; the reviewed fixtures are covered, but future player-mode reason sources should keep adding stable player-copy keys rather than relying on fallback output.
The R2 fix should remain copy/flow-level and must not expand into final art, manual node battle, new domain rules, or new command surfaces.
blockers: Fix blocked-route assistant behavior so the displayed next action is executable or explicitly tells the player what to do when Review obligations is disabled.
Replace the player-facing raw/read-model wording in the right inspector with gameplay copy tied to obligations, supply, governance, and campaign planning.
requested_action: Prepare R2 for the two gameplay/UX issues above, then reroute to gameplay_designer before test_engineer/qa_reviewer. Do not send this implementation to QA yet.
